name: Table Mountain
summary: Table Mountain is a flat-topped mountain overlooking Cape Town, formed over 600 million years ago. It is one of the oldest mountains on Earth and home to an extraordinary diversity of plant life, with over 1,470 species of fynbos. Designated as one of the New7Wonders of Nature in 2011, Table Mountain is South Africa's most photographed landmark.

timeline.title: First Recorded Ascent
timeline.description: Portuguese navigator Antonio de Saldanha becomes the first European to make a recorded ascent of Table Mountain, climbing it to survey Table Bay below. He originally named it Taboa do Cabo (Table of the Cape) after the flat summit. The mountain's distinctive flat top, approximately 3 kilometers wide, is the result of sandstone erosion over hundreds of millions of years.

timeline.title: Dutch Settlement at the Cape
timeline.description: Jan van Riebeeck of the Dutch East India Company establishes a refreshment station at the foot of Table Mountain for ships traveling between Europe and Asia. The mountain becomes an essential navigational landmark for sailors rounding the Cape of Good Hope. European settlers begin to systematically record the mountain's remarkable plant diversity, eventually cataloging the unique Cape floral kingdom.

timeline.title: Construction of the First Path
timeline.description: The first formal hiking path to the summit of Table Mountain is constructed, making the peak accessible to recreational visitors for the first time. The path, built by Thomas Maclear, follows the Platteklip Gorge route. Before this, reaching the summit required technical climbing skills. The construction signals the beginning of Table Mountain's transformation from a wild landscape into a recreational destination.

timeline.title: Table Mountain Aerial Cableway Opens
timeline.description: The Table Mountain Aerial Cableway is officially opened on October 4, 1929, carrying passengers 1,085 meters to the summit in approximately 10 minutes. The original cable car could hold 25 passengers and was designed by a Swedish engineering firm. The cableway revolutionizes access to the mountain, and by its 80th anniversary, it has carried over 22 million visitors to the top.

timeline.title: Table Mountain National Park Established
timeline.description: Table Mountain National Park is officially established, encompassing not only the mountain itself but also the Cape of Good Hope Nature Reserve and a chain of coastal areas stretching from Signal Hill to Cape Point. The park protects 221 square kilometers of extraordinary biodiversity, including the critically endangered Cape Peninsula mountain ghost frog and over 70 percent of the Cape Floral Kingdom's threatened plant species.

timeline.title: UNESCO Cape Floral Region Recognition
timeline.description: Table Mountain and the broader Cape Floral Region are inscribed as a UNESCO World Heritage Site, recognized for their outstanding universal value as one of the world's six floral kingdoms. The Cape Floral Region contains nearly 20 percent of Africa's total flora within an area covering just 0.04 percent of the continent. Table Mountain alone has more plant species than the entire British Isles.

timeline.title: New7Wonders of Nature
timeline.description: Table Mountain is officially named one of the New7Wonders of Nature following a global poll involving hundreds of millions of votes. The designation places Table Mountain alongside the Amazon Rainforest, Halong Bay, Iguazu Falls, Jeju Island, Komodo Island, and the Puerto Princesa Underground River. The announcement is celebrated across South Africa and significantly boosts tourism to Cape Town.
